seoTitle: How to Fix Outlook Search Not Working (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Fix Outlook search returning zero results by rebuilding the Windows search index, running a Quick Repair, and checking indexing location settings.
intro: A frozen or empty search bar in Outlook can make it feel impossible to find an old client thread. Search problems are usually caused by a corrupted local index rather than a missing email. This guide covers rebuilding the index, running Office's repair tool, and verifying Outlook is included in your search locations.

steps.heading: Confirm the problem
steps.body: Click into your search bar, type a common keyword, and check if the interface shows an indexing status alert.

steps.heading: Open Windows Indexing Options
steps.body: Close Outlook, open the Windows Start Menu, search for Indexing Options, and open it.

steps.heading: Verify Outlook is included
steps.body: Review the list of included locations and confirm Microsoft Outlook is explicitly listed.

steps.heading: Rebuild the index
steps.body: Click the advanced button, find the index settings section, and click Rebuild, then confirm the prompt.

steps.heading: Run a Quick Repair
steps.body: Alternatively, open Settings, go to Apps, find Microsoft 365, click Advanced options, and select Quick Repair to patch broken application files.

steps.heading: Verify with a fresh search
steps.body: Relaunch Outlook and run a search query to confirm results now load correctly.

faq.question: How long does rebuilding the search index take?
faq.answer: It can take anywhere from a few minutes to several hours depending on how much mail you have, and search may be incomplete until it finishes.

faq.question: What if Outlook isn't listed in Indexing Options at all?
faq.answer: You'll need to add it manually to the included locations before rebuilding the index.

faq.question: Is Quick Repair the same as reinstalling Office?
faq.answer: No, Quick Repair patches broken application files without a full reinstall, making it a faster first step.

faq.question: Could my IT administrator be blocking search indexing?
faq.answer: Yes, for enterprise accounts, a tenant administrator may have disabled local search indexing through group policy.
